Maybe they've just started dabbling in cursor or clawed code. they're actively trying to invest in this part of their practice as a designer, but it still feels kind of early. Anything you want to share with them before I let you go?
turns.zh: 在你离开之前，我想借鉴你的经验，你是一个真正能够跨越设计和工程这两个领域的人。你有丰富的编程背景，正在使用这些工具，推动事情向前发展。那么，对于在这条路上走得没那么远的设计师，你有什么经验教训或建议吗？也许他们刚刚开始尝试 Cursor 或 Claude Code，正在积极尝试将这部分作为设计师实践的一部分进行投资，但感觉还处于早期阶段。在我放你走之前，你有什么想对他们说的吗？

turns.en: I think the thing I would say is something that applies to me, too, which is that when I'm using AI tools, I'm always trying to think about how can I be picking up stuff as I go and learning from this experience and getting more in the details faster using the AI rather than staying away from the details.
turns.zh: 我想说的是，这也适用于我自己：当我使用 AI 工具时，我总是在思考如何边做边学，从经验中学习，并利用 AI 更快地深入细节，而不是远离细节。

transcript.sec: Active effort in using AI for learning

turns.en: And I think it requires active effort to use AI tools in this way, but it is absolutely possible to do it. So concrete example, maybe when I ask Claude to do something, sometimes they'll say, 'Give me the plan and then I'll go do it.' Then if I get stuck, I'll ask you for help. And I'm doing that intentionally because that's forcing me to go through the friction of actually learning what's going on here. And maybe I'll have it do stuff I already know how to do. But for stuff that I don't know how to do, I'll do it myself, right? And I think for anyone on any point on the technical spectrum, there's always new stuff to learn, always new things to pick up. We have the best learning machines ever invented now, but they happen to also be cheat on your homework machines at the same time. And so every day we get to make a choice. Are we going to cheat on our homework? Are we going to get better?
turns.zh: 我认为以这种方式使用 AI 工具需要主动努力，但这绝对是可以做到的。举个具体例子，当我让 Claude 做某事时，有时它会说：‘给我计划，然后我去执行。’如果我卡住了，我会向它求助。我故意这样做，因为这迫使我去经历摩擦，真正理解发生了什么。我可能会让它做我已经会的事情，但对于我不会的事情，我会自己动手。我认为对于技术光谱上的任何人来说，总有新东西要学，总有新东西要掌握。我们现在拥有了有史以来最好的学习机器，但它们同时也成了帮你作弊的机器。所以我们每天都要做出选择：我们是要作弊，还是要变得更好？

turns.en: Something I've been very aware of recently is the difference between when I'm using Cursor and Claude Code in default settings where I have to approve everything. You know, like Claude Code out of the box kind of slows you down in a way that maybe I don't know, maybe that's how it ends with flow state, but it is that's learning mode for me, you know, like I'm trying to get a little bit more intentional about, okay, you know what, I'm actually going to read this. Like, I'm going to try to make sense of this, maybe ask a follow-up question, and then I'll hit approve rather than sometimes in Cursor, it's a little bit easy to kind of just start scrolling more quickly down the chat history.
turns.zh: 我最近非常注意一件事，就是我在使用 Cursor 和 Claude Code 默认设置（需要我批准所有操作）时的区别。Claude Code 开箱即用会以某种方式让你慢下来，也许这会影响心流状态，但对我来说这就是学习模式。我试着更有意识地去做：好吧，我实际上要读这个，我要试着理解它，也许问个后续问题，然后才点批准。而在 Cursor 里，有时很容易就开始快速滚动聊天记录。

turns.en: Yeah, that's funny. I mean, I would even push further on that and say, 'So, yes, approving commands is one way, but can we do better, right? So, maybe it just goes and does the whole thing, and then it comes back to you with some learning experience. It knows your context. It knows how much you know, and it's going to try to teach you how this thing works. So, what if it like puts together a 10-minute video teaching you how it works with interactive diagrams and then quiz questions that you have to answer to check for understanding, right? Like, why aren't we doing that?' I mean, it's that sort of stuff is on the edge of current capabilities, but I think it's absolutely doable and we should be demanding really, really good explanations of what the AI did that bring us along as humans. And I think, you know, that's a very underexplored area.
turns.zh: 对，这很有趣。我甚至想更进一步：批准命令是一种方式，但我们能做得更好吗？也许它直接做完所有事情，然后带着学习体验回来找你。它知道你的背景，知道你知道多少，它会试图教你这个东西是如何工作的。比如，它制作一个 10 分钟的视频，用交互式图表教你工作原理，然后出一些测验题让你回答以检查理解程度。我们为什么不做这个？这些东西处于当前能力的边缘，但我认为绝对可行，我们应该要求 AI 给出非常非常好的解释，让我们人类能跟上。这是一个非常未被探索的领域。

turns.en: Wow. I hadn't really thought about that before. And immediately the picture in my brain was almost like TikTok style, you know, like just make it as engaging as possible. Show me what you did and have me participate in that ideation process. That is very there's so much in that world.
turns.zh: 哇，我之前真没想过这个。我脑海里立刻浮现出类似 TikTok 风格的画面，就是尽可能让它有吸引力。向我展示你做了什么，让我参与那个构思过程。那个世界里有太多东西了。

turns.en: And there are simpler versions of it too. Every time I review a code PR now, whether it's one by a human or an AI, I ask AI for help reviewing it. So I'll say what's basically going on here. Give me an overview. What file should I read in what order? What are some things that I should look out for? You know, AI can help us be more critical with our thinking, not less. Which I'm sure that you are in the throngs of experiencing that at Notion onboarding to a very large codebase.
turns.zh: 也有更简单的版本。现在每次我审查代码 PR，无论是人类写的还是 AI 写的，我都会请 AI 帮忙审查。我会问：这里大致在做什么？给我一个概述。我应该按什么顺序读哪些文件？有哪些需要注意的地方？AI 可以帮助我们更批判性地思考，而不是更少。我相信你在 Notion 入职一个非常大的代码库时，正在经历这一点。

turns.en: This is a huge thing for me onboarding this codebase like it is very in my first months at the company. It's really useful to me to actually learn how the damn thing works.
turns.zh: 这对我入职这个代码库来说非常重要，尤其是在我入职公司的头几个月。真正学会这东西怎么运作对我非常有用。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 对。

turns.en: Rather than just like have everything done for me, right? So, it's both like a huge accelerant and like a bit of a dangerous crutch if I don't use it the right way, I think.
turns.zh: 而不是什么都替我做好。所以它既是巨大的加速器，如果使用不当，也有点像危险的拐杖。

turns.en: Well, Geoffrey, you've left me with a lot to think about. I really appreciate you coming on and taking the time today to share some of the things that are rattling around in your brain and congrats on the role at Notion. Very excited to see what you bring into the world.
turns.zh: 好吧，Geoffrey，你让我思考了很多。非常感谢你今天来分享你脑子里的一些想法，祝贺你在 Notion 的新职位。非常期待你带给世界的东西。

turns.en: Thanks so much. Great to talk to you.
turns.zh: 非常感谢。很高兴和你聊天。

turns.en: Before I let you go, I want to take just one minute to run you through my favorite products because I'm constantly asked what's in my stack. Framer is how I build websites. Genway is how I do research. Granola is how I take notes during crit. Jitter is how I animate my designs. Lovable is how I build my ideas in code. Mobin is how I find design inspiration. Paper is how I design like a creative. And Raycast is my shortcut every step of the way. Now, I've hand selected these companies so that I can do these episodes full-time. So, by far the number one way to support the show is to check them out. You can find the full list at dive.club/partners.
turns.zh: 在结束之前，我想花一分钟介绍我最喜欢的产品，因为我经常被问到我的工具栈。Framer 是我建站的方式，Genway 是我做研究的方式，Granola 是我在评审时记笔记的方式，Jitter 是我给设计做动画的方式，Lovable 是我用代码实现想法的方式，Mobin 是我找设计灵感的方式，Paper 是我像创意人一样设计的方式，Raycast 是我每一步的快捷方式。我亲手挑选了这些公司，这样我才能全职做这些节目。目前支持节目的首要方式就是去看看它们。你可以在 dive.club/partners 找到完整列表。
